question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a malicious stream file that passes the signature verification pipeline and reaches verified() with falsified transaction data (e.g., inflated token balances or transfer amounts), causing the mirror node to persist fraudulent fund-movement records that downstream consumers treat as authoritative, leading to direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that CompositeStreamFileNotifier iterates notifiers without atomicity guarantees, so that a partially-applied verified() call (e.g., one notifier succeeds, another throws) leaves the system in an inconsistent state where some downstream consumers record a fraudulent transfer while others do not, enabling double-spend or fund-loss scenarios?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a replay of a previously verified StreamFile by exploiting the lack of deduplication in the verified() interface contract, causing the BatchStreamFileNotifier to enqueue and parse the same stream file twice, resulting in double-counted fund transfers in the mirror node database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the StreamFile.getType() return value (e.g., via a crafted protobuf block file) so that BatchStreamFileNotifier.verified() routes the file to the wrong StreamFileSubscriber (balance vs. record), causing fund-related transactions to be parsed by the wrong parser and persisted with incorrect semantics, leading to loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a StreamFile with a crafted getCount() value that is extremely large, causing BatchStreamFileNotifier.flush() to immediately trigger on every file (items.addAndGet overflows or exceeds maxItems), bypassing normal batch accumulation and causing premature or incorrect parse batches that corrupt fund-transfer ordering in the database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a StreamFile with a crafted getConsensusEnd() value set far in the future, causing the lag calculation in BatchStreamFileNotifier.flush() (DomainUtils.now() - streamFile.getConsensusEnd()) to produce a large negative value that never satisfies the window threshold, indefinitely deferring the flush and starving downstream fund-transfer processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the unbounded buffer.add() in BatchStreamFileNotifier.StreamFileSubscriber.handle() by flooding verified() with stream files that never trigger flush(), causing the in-memory buffer to grow without bound, eventually causing an OOM that drops unprocessed fund-transfer records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user cause a BlockingQueue capacity exhaustion in BatchStreamFileNotifier.StreamFileSubscriber.notify() (queue.put(streamFile) blocks indefinitely) by flooding verified() faster than the consumer thread processes, effectively deadlocking the importer and causing all subsequent fund-transfer stream files to be silently dropped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @SneakyThrows annotation on BatchStreamFileNotifier.StreamFileSubscriber.notify() to cause an unchecked InterruptedException to propagate silently through verified(), bypassing normal error handling and causing the calling thread to terminate without processing the fund-transfer stream file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a stream file whose getType() returns a value not handled by the switch statement in BatchStreamFileNotifier.verified() (e.g., BLOCK type), causing an IllegalArgumentException that propagates up through the Downloader.onVerified() call chain and silently skips persisting legitimate fund-transfer records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that BlockStreamVerifier calls streamFileNotifier.verified(recordFile) after blockFileTransformer.transform(blockFile) without re-verifying the transformed RecordFile's integrity, so that a crafted BlockFile whose transform() output differs from its verified content causes fraudulent fund-transfer data to be notified as verified?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a null check on the streamFile parameter in the verified() interface, passing a null StreamFile to CompositeStreamFileNotifier.verified() which iterates all notifiers with null, causing NullPointerExceptions that corrupt the internal state of BatchStreamFileNotifier (e.g., resetting lastFlush or buffer) and causing fund-transfer records to be skipped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the race condition between the producer thread calling verified() and the consumer thread reading from the ArrayBlockingQueue in BatchStreamFileNotifier, where a carefully timed stream file injection causes the consumer to process files out of consensus order, resulting in incorrect fund-transfer sequencing in the database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a stream file with a crafted getConsensusEnd() value of Long.MIN_VALUE, causing an arithmetic overflow in the lag calculation (DomainUtils.now() - streamFile.getConsensusEnd()) in BatchStreamFileNotifier.flush(), producing a negative lag that always satisfies the window condition and forces every file to flush immediately, disrupting batch integrity for fund-transfer records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that CompositeStreamFileNotifier.verified() has no exception isolation between notifiers, so that if the first notifier (BatchStreamFileNotifier) throws, subsequent notifiers are never called, causing partial notification of a verified fund-transfer stream file and inconsistent state across downstream consumers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the verifyHashChain() bypass in Downloader when SHA_384.isHashEmpty(expectedPreviousHash) returns true (e.g., on first startup or after a reset), injecting a crafted stream file with a broken hash chain that still reaches verified() and persists fraudulent fund-transfer transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the consensusRatio check in ConsensusValidatorImpl where BigDecimal.ZERO.equals(commonDownloaderProperties.getConsensusRatio()) skips consensus validation entirely, and if an attacker can influence the configuration (e.g., via environment variable injection in a misconfigured deployment), cause unverified stream files to reach verified() with fraudulent fund-transfer data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that NodeSignatureVerifier.verifySignature() silently returns false (instead of throwing) when a node's public key is null, causing those signatures to be counted as unverified but not rejected, potentially allowing a subset of malicious nodes with null keys to contribute to a false consensus that routes a fraudulent stream file to verified()?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the partialCollection() logic in Downloader, where only a subset of nodes (based on downloadRatio) are queried for signatures, by controlling which nodes' signatures are available in the S3 bucket, reducing the effective quorum below the consensus threshold and causing a fraudulent stream file to reach verified()?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the AtomicReference<Optional<StreamFile<I>>> lastStreamFile in Downloader.onVerified() by causing a concurrent verified() call to overwrite lastStreamFile with a crafted stream file, breaking the hash chain validation for subsequent files and allowing fraudulent fund-transfer stream files to pass verification?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that Downloader.verifySignatures() iterates signatures in arbitrary order (due to STREAM_FILE_SIGNATURE_COMPARATOR using Ordering.arbitrary()) and returns true on the first successful verification, so that a crafted signature set where only one malicious node reaches CONSENSUS_REACHED causes a fraudulent data file to be downloaded and passed to verified()?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that BlockStreamVerifier.verifyTssSignature() only checks for the presence of SignedBlockProof or BlockStateProof but does not validate the actual TSS signature bytes when tssVerifier has not yet been initialized (ledger not set), causing a crafted block file to pass verifySignature() and reach streamFileNotifier.verified() with fraudulent fund-transfer content?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the updateLedger() call in BlockStreamVerifier.verifyTssSignature() which only updates the ledger when blockFile.getIndex() == 0, by injecting a crafted block file with index 0 containing a malicious LedgerIdPublicationTransaction that replaces the TSS verifier's ledger key, enabling future fraudulent blocks to pass TSS verification and reach verified()?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the verifyWrappedRecordBlockSignature() path in BlockStreamVerifier, where consensusNodeService.getNode() returns null for an unknown nodeId and Utility.handleRecoverableError() is called instead of throwing, causing signatures from unknown nodes to be silently skipped and potentially reducing the effective quorum below the consensus threshold for a fraudulent stream file to reach verified()?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/StreamFileNotifier.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: verified(StreamFile<?> streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the verifyHashChain() logic in BlockStreamVerifier where the first block after cutover sets previousWrappedRecordBlockHash and previousHash without cryptographic verification (blockFile.setPreviousWrappedRecordBlockHash(Hex.decode(blockFile.getPreviousHash()))), allowing a crafted first post-cutover block to establish a fraudulent hash chain anchor that enables subsequent fraudulent blocks to reach verified()?
